description:
## Problem

Today `c11 trigger-flash --surface <ref>` flashes only the pane content in-place, briefly. Two issues stack:

1. When the surface is in a non-focused workspace or tab, the flash is invisible, defeating the purpose of "hey, look here." The sidebar tab selector for that workspace/surface should flash too.
2. Even when visible, the flash is too brief and singular to catch attention reliably from across the screen. The operator running 10+ surfaces wants something that says "look at THIS one" with conviction, and stays said until acknowledged.

## Asks

1. **Sidebar tab flash, in addition to the pane.** When `trigger-flash` fires for a surface, also flash the corresponding sidebar tab item. Treat the flash as an addressable attention signal across all representations of the surface, not just its pane rect.

2. **Persistent flash mode.** New optional flag (e.g. `--persistent` on `c11 trigger-flash`) that makes the flash continue (pulsing) until the operator clicks the surface to dismiss. Clicking either the pane content or the corresponding sidebar tab cancels the flash. The c11 socket should expose a programmatic cancel as well (e.g. `c11 cancel-flash --surface <ref>`) for agents that want to clear the signal without simulating a click. Default behavior remains one-shot for backward compatibility.

3. **Default flash color, configurable.** Default flash color is yellow (warm, hard-to-miss). Allow override per call (e.g. `--color <hex>`) and via a theme key (`flash.color`, optionally `flash.sidebarColor` if they should differ) so themes, projects, or agents can pick distinguishable signals.

4. **Full flash across both pane content and sidebar tab.** The visual should wrap both the pane content area AND the sidebar tab item, treating them as one signal: same color, same envelope, applied to each representation. Not just a tab-corner indicator and a separate pane tint, a coherent "this surface is calling you" treatment.

5. **Longer one-shot tab flash, configurable.** Even in non-persistent (one-shot) mode the sidebar tab flash should run noticeably longer than the current default, long enough that an operator scanning a busy sidebar registers it. Expose the duration as a setting (Settings → Notifications → Tab Flash Duration, or similar) so power users can tune it.

## Context

- Discovered during a conversation about building a c11 theming engine. Description rewritten 2026-05-05 to broaden the asks (persistence, default color, longer tab flash, click-to-dismiss).
- Ties into the broader theming effort, since flash is a chrome-level attribute that wants to be theme-driven, but the persistence and duration mechanics ship independently of any theming plan.

## Suggested relationship

Mark `related_to` the theming epic and the pane title bar work (CMUX-12) when convenient. Persistent-flash and the duration setting should land independently of any theme engine work; the color theming key can layer on top later.

## Non-goals

- Don't flash the Ghostty terminal cell content; that's the renderer's domain. Flash is a chrome-level signal applied around the pane and on the tab.
- Don't flash the macOS window frame or traffic lights.
- No system-wide attention sound; flash is purely visual.
title: Persistent, themable surface flash across pane and sidebar tab
